title: Member Import Tutorial
subtitle: Learn how to bulk-import members into your organisation using a CSV file.

section_overview.heading: Overview
section_overview.body: The member import feature allows organisation administrators to add multiple members in one step by uploading a CSV file. The system creates user accounts, links them to the organisation, and creates their formal membership record automatically. Existing members are updated, not duplicated.

section_requirements.heading: What does the CSV file need?
section_requirements.intro: The file must contain at minimum one column:
section_requirements.optional_heading: Optional columns (all improve the quality of imported data):
optional: membership_number — custom membership ID (auto-generated if empty)
joined_at — date the person joined, e.g. 2024-01-15
status — active, expired, suspended, or ended (defaults to active)
fees_status — paid, unpaid, partial, or exempt (defaults to unpaid)

section_file_format.heading: CSV File Format
section_file_format.intro: The file must be a CSV with comma (,) or semicolon (;) as the delimiter — the system detects it automatically.
rules: The first row must be a header row with column names exactly as listed above.
Each subsequent row represents one member.
Dates must follow the format YYYY-MM-DD (e.g. 2024-01-15).
The file must be saved in UTF-8 encoding.
Maximum file size: 50 MB. Supports up to 50,000 members per import.
section_file_format.download_hint: Use the 'Download Template' button on the import page to get a pre-formatted CSV file with sample data.

section_upsert.heading: New vs Existing Members
section_upsert.body: The import intelligently handles both new and existing members:

items.label: New email (not on platform)
items.description: A new user account is created, linked to the organisation, and a member record is added.

items.label: Existing platform user (not yet a member here)
items.description: Their existing account is reused — no duplicate user is created. They are linked to this organisation and a member record is added.

items.label: Already a member of this organisation
items.description: Their member record is updated with the new values from the CSV (status, fees_status, expires_at, joined_at, membership_number). Nothing is duplicated.

steps.title: Step 1 — Prepare your CSV file
steps.body: Download the template from the import page and fill in your member data. Make sure the email column is present and all dates use the YYYY-MM-DD format.

steps.title: Step 2 — Upload the file
steps.body: On the import page, click 'Select File' or drag and drop your CSV file into the upload area.

steps.title: Step 3 — Review the preview
steps.body: The system shows the first 10 rows and any validation warnings. If the data looks correct, proceed to import.

steps.title: Step 4 — Start the import
steps.body: Click 'Import'. Large files are processed in the background — you can close the tab and come back later. A progress bar shows how many rows have been processed.

steps.title: Step 5 — View the result
steps.body: Once complete, you will see how many members were imported and how many were skipped. Visit the Members list to verify the results.

items.question: What happens if I import the same file twice?
items.answer: Existing members are updated, not duplicated. Running the same import twice is safe.

items.question: What does 'skipped' mean in the results?
items.answer: A row is skipped if the email column is empty. All other rows are either imported (new) or updated (existing).

items.question: Can I update only the expiry date for existing members?
items.answer: Yes. Include only the email and expires_at columns. Existing members will have their expiry date updated. Fields left empty in the CSV are not overwritten.

items.question: What membership status should I use?
items.answer: Use 'active' for current members, 'expired' for lapsed memberships, 'suspended' for temporarily blocked members, and 'ended' for permanently terminated memberships. If omitted, the system defaults to 'active'.

items.question: What happens to a member's voting rights after import?
items.answer: Only members with status 'active' and a membership type that grants voting rights can vote in elections. You can assign a membership type from the Members page after import.

items.question: My import shows 0 imported — what went wrong?
items.answer: Check that the CSV has a header row with an 'email' column, that emails are valid, and that the file is saved in UTF-8 encoding. Download the template as a reference.

seo.title: Member Import Tutorial | Public Digit
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to importing organisation members via CSV on Public Digit. Learn required columns, date formats, upsert rules, and how to handle existing members.
seo.keywords: member import tutorial, CSV member import, bulk member upload, organisation member management, Public Digit members
